completed: Add all critical MongoDB indexes (NodueApproval x6, NodueRequest x3, Student rollNo unique + compound, Faculty departmentId+roleTags)
Upgrade DB config: minPoolSize:2, serverSelectionTimeoutMS, socketTimeoutMS
Install compression middleware; wire into app.js
Rewrite approvalController.js: surgical cache invalidation after every action, .select() on all queries
Rewrite batchController.js: bulkWrite for initiation, getBatchStatus cached 60s
Rewrite hodController.js: getOverview cached 60s, overrideDues invalidates 3 keys
Fix server.js: await connectDB() before app.listen()
Implemented rate limiting middleware with tiers: apiLimiter, authLimiter, importLimiter, healthLimiter.

Fixed HoD portal not reflecting class-teacher/mentor/subject approvals — all approval actions now invalidate hod_overview:<deptId> cache key immediately after commit (approveRequest, markDue, updateApproval, bulkApproveRequests, overrideDues, bulkOverrideDues)
pending: Add Cache-Control: private, max-age=300 header to subject list endpoint
Consider prewarmService for deadline-day cache priming

decisions.context: batchController initiateBatch used per-student session loop
decisions.decision: Replace with two parallel bulkWrite calls (requests + approvals collection)
decisions.trade_offs: Loses per-document Mongoose middleware, but those were not used here. Gains ~30x speed.
Slightly more code per mutation, but prevents thundering-herd cache miss storms at peak load.

decisions.decision: Removed populate — use denormalized facultyName field stored on NodueApproval at creation time
decisions.trade_offs: No extra Faculty collection query on the hot path. Name must be kept in sync on faculty rename (rare).

decisions.context: HoD overview cache not busting after class-teacher/mentor/faculty approvals
decisions.decision: All approval mutations now call invalidateKeys('hod_overview:<deptId>') immediately after transaction commit
decisions.trade_offs: Requires batch.departmentId in every approval function — already fetched for auth checks, so zero extra DB round-trips.
